title: Reduce dynamic power at the activity source
description: Cut switching activity through gating, operand isolation, and avoiding needless toggling on wide buses.
body: Use when dynamic power dominates the budget on an active design. Do not chase only clock frequency, since switching activity and capacitance also drive dynamic power. Actions: profile switching activity, apply operand isolation to datapaths that compute unused results, and gate clocks on idle banks. Validation signals include a lower activity factor in power analysis and reduced dynamic power without functional change. Risk boundary: an aggressive activity reduction can break timing or function, so escalate to verification when gating logic grows complex.
